text: This article deals with the explanation of the first commandment as it appears in the Heidelberg Catechism (HC). The reconstructed German and Latin texts appear in the introduction. An explanation of the possible background of Zacharias Ursinus, the main author of the HC, is attempted as Ursinus was a student of Philipp Melanchthon in Wittenberg. Two of Melanchthon’s works have been re-examined in an endeavour to determine his theological background and thus an implied background for the HK. An explanation of the explanations given to the first commandment in Ursinus’ Small and Large Catechisms, and some insights from his dogmatic lectures are also attempted with the aim of understanding better the explanation of the first commandment in the HK. Die artikel fokus op die uitleg van die eerste gebod in die Heidelbergse Kategismus (HK). Rekonstruksies van die oorspronklike Duitse en Latynse tekste word voorgelê. Zacharias Ursinus, die primêre outeur van die HK, was ’n leerling van Philipp Melanchthon in Wittenberg. Twee belangrike publikasies van Melanchthon is nagevors in die soeke na die teologiese denkwêreld as agtergrond tot die HK. Ursinus se uitleg van die eerste gebod in sy Klein en Groot Kategismusse, asook sommige van die insigte wat in sy die dogmatieklesings vervat is, word verduidelik in ’n poging om ’n beter begrip van die uitleg van die HK teweeg te bring.
